image.alt: Mughal miniature of a young ruler with a golden halo, holding a falcon on his hand, in a yellow robe

rounds.revealFact: Akbar expanded the Mughal Empire across much of the Indian subcontinent and was renowned for his administrative reforms and unusual religious tolerance.

image.alt: Aged painting of a walled mission compound on open ground, with columns of soldiers advancing on its walls

clues: A few hundred defenders held a fortified mission for nearly two weeks against an army many times their size.
Their last stand became a rallying cry that fueled a wider war for a territory's independence.

pairs.a: Samuel Morse sends his first long-distance telegraph message
pairs.b: The first transatlantic telegraph cable links Europe and North America

pairs.a: The first commercial radio station begins regular broadcasts
The first communications satellite relays a live television signal
pairs.b: The first message is sent over the ARPANET

pairs.a: The World Wide Web is opened to the public
pairs.b: The first hand-held mobile phone call is made

pairs.a: The first daily newspaper is printed in England
pairs.b: The first postage stamp, the Penny Black, is issued

image.alt: Black-and-white photo of a four-engine propeller plane flying low over a crowd of children standing on a mound of rubble

options: The fall of the Berlin Wall

followUpQuestion.prompt: Which power had cut off the city by land, forcing supplies to come by air?

rounds.prompt: President Lincoln's Emancipation Proclamation declares enslaved people in the rebelling states to be free.
rounds.revealFact: The Emancipation Proclamation reframed the American Civil War as a struggle to end slavery and opened the Union Army to Black soldiers.

rounds.prompt: Where did a Bronze Age civilization build a sprawling palace of painted halls and twisting corridors, remembered in legend as a labyrinth?
rounds.revealFact: Knossos was the largest palace of the Minoan civilization on Crete; its maze-like plan may have inspired the Greek legend of the Labyrinth and the Minotaur.
